articles.headline: China steps up scrutiny of indium exports, raising supply-chain risk for data-center hardware
articles.summary: Reuters reports that Beijing is increasing scrutiny over indium exports, a niche metal used in next-generation data centers and electronics. If expanded into formal export controls, the move could affect hardware supply chains, component availability, and planning for infrastructure-heavy businesses.[4]

articles.headline: SpaceX reportedly acquires AI coding startup Cursor in $60 billion deal
articles.summary: SpaceX’s reported acquisition of Anysphere, the maker of Cursor, signals a major bet on AI software tools for engineering productivity and could reshape demand for developer platforms. The same report says the deal helped lift tech-linked stocks, making this relevant for companies tracking AI valuations, software spend, and market sentiment.[1]

articles.headline: DeepSeek becomes China’s most valuable AI startup after $7.4 billion fundraise
articles.summary: DeepSeek’s large fundraising round underscores how quickly capital is flowing into frontier AI in China, with implications for competition, pricing pressure, and model development. For executives, the bigger issue is that this reinforces global AI rivalry and may influence cloud, chip, and application investment decisions.[2]
